hot_topics.topic: The Evolving Landscape of AI Development, Ethics, and Societal Impact
hot_topics.synthesis: The rapid advancement and integration of AI continue to drive significant discussions around development, ethical implications, and societal impact. New models and tools are emerging for coding and creative tasks, yet concerns persist regarding AI misuse for generating harmful content, academic cheating, and algorithmic bias in platforms like TikTok. Regulatory frameworks and privacy safeguards are being explored to address these challenges, while the economic effects, such as soaring housing prices in tech hubs, are becoming increasingly evident.

hot_topics.topic: Political Turmoil and Succession in the US Senate Race (Graham Platner)
hot_topics.synthesis: The US Senate race in Maine is in disarray following Democrat Graham Platner's withdrawal amidst a sexual assault allegation, which he denies. His fiery exit, blaming the "establishment" for undermining his campaign, has exposed deep rifts within the Democratic Party. Multiple candidates are now vying to replace him, with the state party working to establish a convention process for nomination, highlighting the critical nature of this "must-win" race.

hot_topics.topic: Autonomous Vehicles Face Regulatory Scrutiny and Public Incidents
hot_topics.synthesis: Robotaxi companies like Waymo are facing increased scrutiny regarding their operations and interactions with the public. While Waymo offers free rides in certain California expansion areas due to regulatory delays, incidents involving misbehaving passengers being reported to police by robotaxis have surfaced. Simultaneously, federal regulators are demanding that autonomous vehicle companies ensure their vehicles do not interfere with first responders, emphasizing the need for robust safety protocols in complex real-world scenarios.

hot_topics.topic: Escalating Tensions and Energy Impacts in the Middle East
hot_topics.synthesis: Recent attacks and heightened tensions in the Middle East are causing significant disruptions, notably a decline in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine continues to strike Russian ships and fuel supplies near Crimea, while the US considers providing Patriot missile licenses to Ukraine. Geopolitical instability is also accelerating a global shift towards clean energy, highlighting the precarity of reliance on fossil fuels.

hot_topics.topic: Legal Challenges and Ethics in Prediction Markets
hot_topics.synthesis: Prediction markets like Kalshi are encountering legal and ethical challenges, particularly regarding state gambling laws and insider trading. A federal judge upheld New York's right to regulate Kalshi, which plans to appeal, while campaign staffers are still attempting to bet on political races, prompting calls for stricter controls despite existing safeguards.

hot_topics.topic: Vandalism of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
hot_topics.synthesis: A former US Olympian canoeist has pleaded not guilty to charges of deliberately damaging the lining of the refurbished Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. The individual, David Hearn, faces property destruction charges related to the alleged act of vandalism.

items.title: Africa’s cultural landmarks: rock-hewn churches of Lalibela, Ethiopia
items.summary: Ethiopia's rock-hewn churches of Lalibela stand as unique cultural landmarks, carved top-to-bottom from single rock formations rather than built conventionally. These wondrous structures showcase an extraordinary architectural feat.

items.title: How to offset your brain
items.summary: Skillfully targeted mindfulness practices can help individuals overcome common cognitive biases like confirmation bias and loss aversion. Everyone is susceptible to these biases, and mindfulness offers a method for counteracting them.

items.title: Aussie gov't tells volunteers to throw out thousands of functioning test routers
items.summary: Thousands of SamKnows routers distributed to volunteers for Australia's Measuring Broadband Australia program have been bricked after the government initiative concluded. Participants were instructed to dispose of the fully functional devices, raising questions about resource waste.

items.title: Conspiracies and regrets abound in Dune: Part Three trailer
items.summary: A new trailer for Denis Villeneuve’s Dune: Part Three has been released, offering tantalizing hints and Easter eggs for fans of the book series. The film continues the story of Paul Atreides after the brutal defeat of House Atreides, as he seeks refuge with the Fremen alongside his mother.

items.title: Free Waymo rides in California? You can thank a regulatory quirk.
items.summary: Due to a delayed regulatory decision in California, Waymo is currently unable to charge passengers for rides in its new Ojai robotaxi vehicles in certain expansion areas. While the company charges for its Jaguar I-Pace fleet, the Ojai rides could remain gratis until at least September or longer, highlighting California's stricter oversight compared to other states.

items.title: Google pays $250K for Linux vulnerability allowing guest VM escapes
items.summary: Google paid $250,000 for a high-severity Linux vulnerability in KVM that allows untrusted guest virtual machines to gain root access to host machines, a flaw unnoticed for 16 years. This "Januscape" vulnerability, affecting both AMD and Intel processors, poses a significant threat to cloud platforms by enabling VM escapes.

items.title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
items.summary: Google has significantly updated Android Bench, its evaluation platform for LLMs in Android app development, adding new models and a more user-friendly framework. The benchmark helps developers identify which AI agents perform best on a suite of 100 development tasks, though Google's own Gemini models still lag behind competitors.

items.title: Judge rejects Kalshi attempt to override New York state gambling laws
items.summary: A federal judge rejected Kalshi's attempt to override New York state gambling laws, preventing the prediction market operator from avoiding state-level enforcement. New York officials lauded the decision, emphasizing consumer protection laws apply to prediction markets, though Kalshi plans to appeal.

items.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
items.summary: A proposed class-action lawsuit against X and xAI alleges that a man used Grok to generate 7,000 child sex images of his stepdaughter, with xAI's safety system only flagging a "gang rape" prompt. The complaint claims xAI built "toxic AI nudify tools" and obstructed police investigations into Grok-generated CSAM.

items.title: Ruf debuts new flat-eight engine at Goodwood
items.summary: Ruf, which has evolved from a Porsche tuner into an independent car manufacturer, debuted a brand-new flat-eight engine at the Goodwood Festival of Speed. The company, known for building its own unique designs and chassis, is expanding beyond its traditional horizontally opposed six-cylinder engines for its next generation of cars.

items.title: Surprised doctors find 10-inch worm in man's groin during elective surgery
items.summary: During an elective inguinal hernia repair, doctors unexpectedly discovered a living 10-inch worm in a man's abdomen, an occurrence the patient reported had happened previously. This unusual finding adds to the complexity of hernia surgeries, which are common for older men but can present rare complications.

items.title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
items.summary: A Brown University economics professor, suspecting AI cheating, found that scores on an in-person final fell by 50% compared to previous take-home assignments. This incident at an Ivy League institution, coupled with a Princeton survey where 29.9% of students admitted AI cheating, reveals a significant problem with academic integrity and the use of generative AI.

items.title: TikTok users don't have as much agency over their FYPs as they think
items.summary: Research suggests TikTok users have less control over their For You Pages than they believe, as negative feedback signals only temporarily affect the algorithm before it gradually relapses. Computer scientists found that consistent and repeated feedback is necessary to significantly alter the content users see.

items.title: Two teens learn the hard way not to do toy gun drive-bys from a Waymo
items.summary: Two California teens were detained by police after a Waymo robotaxi stopped itself and alerted authorities to their misbehavior, which included drinking and shooting gel beads from the vehicle. This incident highlights Waymo's ability to enforce rider rules and report problematic passengers to law enforcement.

items.title: US seeks cheaper hunter-killer drones after Iran destroys $1B worth of Reapers
items.summary: The US military is seeking cheaper "hunter-killer" drones after losing dozens of expensive Reaper drones, valued over $1 billion, to Iranian defenses. This shift aims to deploy more cost-effective drones in large numbers to overwhelm enemy air defenses, mirroring Ukraine's strategy of using inexpensive drones to target Russian supply lines.

items.title: Introducing Plan A
items.summary: "Plan A" is a positive vision and roadmap for navigating the future of AI, proposed by Daniel Kokotajlo and the AI Futures Project, describing a best-of-all-possible-worlds scenario through 2040. A linchpin of this plan is a trustless, joint AI regulatory regime with China, designed to control chip supply and ensure mutual transparency to prevent an uncontrolled intelligence explosion.

items.title: A country that made huge progress on measles now reports 120,000 cases
items.summary: Bangladesh, a country that previously made significant progress on measles, is now reporting 120,000 cases, with hundreds of children dying and hospitals overwhelmed. This resurgence highlights a major public health crisis.

items.title: Arizona toddler found alive in morgue after being declared dead from drowning
items.summary: An 18-month-old toddler in Arizona was found alive in a morgue after being declared dead from drowning, and subsequently survived after being flown to another hospital. This miraculous event highlights a critical medical error.

items.title: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
items.summary: Data indicates a significant drop in oil, gas, and cargo ships using the US-backed Strait of Hormuz route following recent attacks in the waterway. This decline reflects heightened security concerns and disruptions to crucial shipping lanes.

items.title: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
items.summary: Democrat Graham Platner suspended his campaign for a key US Senate race after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an allegation he categorically denies. This decision highlights the immediate impact of such accusations on political candidacies.

items.title: Ex-Olympian pleads not guilty to Reflecting Pool vandalism charges
items.summary: A former US Olympian canoeist pleaded not guilty to charges of vandalizing the refurbished Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. The ex-Olympian denies deliberately damaging the lining of the monument.

items.title: Factory fire kills at least 28 in China's 'shoe capital'
items.summary: A factory fire in China's "shoe capital" has resulted in the deaths of at least 28 people, with footage showing massive flames and thick black smoke. The incident underscores industrial safety concerns in the region.

items.title: Greek F-16 fighter jet crash-lands at busy Zakynthos airport
items.summary: A Greek F-16 fighter jet crash-landed at Zakynthos airport without deploying its landing gear, subsequently bursting into flames and closing the runway. The incident caused significant disruption at the busy airport.

items.title: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
items.summary: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has informed the BBC of a breakthrough in its investigation into war crimes committed in Darfur over the past three years. This development signals progress in efforts to prosecute atrocities in Sudan.

items.title: It was 'love at first sight' with their adopted baby. Then they were told he may have been trafficked
items.summary: A couple's dream of having a child was complicated when they adopted a baby, Marcus, only to be later informed that he may have been trafficked. This devastating news raises the prospect of them losing their adopted son.

items.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for Fifa World Cup final half-time show
items.summary: Justin Bieber is set to join Madonna, Shakira, and BTS for the FIFA World Cup final half-time show, marking his latest major performance since his Coachella comeback. The event is styled after the Super Bowl show, bringing together global music stars.

items.title: Platner's disastrous candidacy exposes rifts that could dampen Democrats' Senate hopes
items.summary: Graham Platner's disastrous candidacy and subsequent exit from a must-win Senate race have exposed deep rifts within the Democratic Party, potentially dampening their Senate hopes. His departure highlights divisions between the party's left wing and moderates.

items.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
items.summary: Labour leader Keir Starmer was gifted a gun and ammunition by the Turkish president at a NATO summit, which has been left with British officials in Turkey. This diplomatic gift raised questions as it was not brought back to the UK.

items.title: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
items.summary: Ukraine has escalated its attacks on Russian fuel supplies by striking Russian ships near Crimea. These strikes appear to be part of an ongoing strategy to disrupt supply lines and routes in and out of occupied Crimea.

items.title: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
items.summary: Donald Trump announced that Ukraine is set to receive a license to produce Patriot missiles, which are highly effective against ballistic missiles but have lengthy production times. This initiative aims to bolster Ukraine's defense capabilities against Russian attacks.

items.title: Watch: Why is there a 'black cloud' over unity at the Nato summit?
items.summary: US President Donald Trump used the NATO summit to criticize a perceived lack of support for his war on Iran and to revive his interest in acquiring Greenland, creating disunity. His statements cast a "black cloud" over the summit's atmosphere of unity.

items.title: Wealthy AI workers send San Francisco house prices soaring
items.summary: The influx of wealthy AI workers is causing San Francisco house prices to soar, with the median cost now reaching a record high of $1.7 million. This trend highlights the significant economic impact of the burgeoning AI industry on local housing markets.

items.title: 'Solo agers' are a growing group. Changes that would help them could help everyone
items.summary: "Solo agers," older adults navigating aging without children or a spouse, are a growing demographic that an expert suggests should be supported rather than viewed as a crisis. Changes designed to help this group could benefit everyone.

items.title: A Florida airport is officially renamed for Trump. What does he stand to gain?
items.summary: A Florida airport has been officially renamed for Donald Trump, making him the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. While the Trump Organization states no royalties will be received, legal experts note potential loopholes.

items.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has died at 75
items.summary: Bonnie Tyler, the singer known for "Total Eclipse of the Heart," has died unexpectedly at 75 in a Portuguese hospital where she was being treated for an illness. Her family released a statement confirming her passing.

items.title: Campaign staffers keep trying to bet on races despite push to curb insider trading
items.summary: Campaign staffers continue attempting to bet on political races despite efforts to curb insider trading on prediction markets like Kalshi, which has blocked "dozens" of trades. Experts suggest loopholes remain, with at least one trade identified by NPR having slipped through.

items.title: Former coach at Bucknell University charged in death of freshman football player
items.summary: Former strength and conditioning coach Mark Kulbis was charged in connection with the death of freshman football player Calvin "CJ" Dickey Jr. at Bucknell University. Dickey, who had sickle-cell trait, collapsed during training.

items.title: Former Olympian pleads not guilty in reflecting pool vandalism charges
items.summary: Former Olympian canoeist David Hearn pleaded not guilty to charges of property destruction exceeding $1,000, related to alleged vandalism of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Hearn denies deliberately damaging the pool's lining.

items.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
items.summary: Democrat Graham Platner ended his U.S. Senate bid following an assault allegation, while civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been engaged to investigate the death of Nolan Wells. The political development occurred amidst other national attention-grabbing news.

items.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
items.summary: Andy Burnham, known for revitalizing Manchester as its mayor, is poised to become the UK's next prime minister, with his working-class background shaping his political approach. His success in bringing growth to a postindustrial city offers a potential blueprint for national leadership.

items.title: New aviation mechanics graduate with jobs in hand, thanks to a labor shortage
items.summary: The US aviation industry faces a significant labor shortage as a generation of mechanics retires, leading to new graduates securing jobs immediately. Record passenger numbers underscore the urgent need to fill this gap in the workforce.

items.title: The Iran war has pushed some countries away from oil and toward clean energy
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly around Iran, is accelerating the global transition away from fossil fuels towards clean energy sources like EVs, solar, and batteries. Experts indicate the war has underscored the instability of relying on fossil fuels.

items.title: Dan Kleban jumps (back) into Maine Senate race
items.summary: Maine Beer Company founder Dan Kleban relaunched his Senate campaign, taking aim at GOP Senator Susan Collins and the "DC establishment" and positioning himself as a candidate to carry forward Platner's movement. He acknowledged the truncated nominating process but stated Maine voters deserve an open selection free from external meddling.

items.title: Inside the tense final hours of Graham Platner’s campaign
items.summary: Graham Platner's campaign ended with a defiant video, blaming "corporate media" and the "political establishment" for his downfall after a sexual assault allegation, despite advisers urging a conciliatory tone. His departure sparked immediate jockeying among potential Democratic replacements in Maine, with the state party outlining a convention process.

items.title: Nirav Shah, governor primary runner-up, jumps into race to replace Platner
items.summary: Nirav Shah, former Maine CDC director and recent gubernatorial primary runner-up, officially entered the race to succeed Graham Platner for the US Senate. Shah, branding himself as a progressive outsider, aims to challenge the "broken system" and defeat incumbent Republican Susan Collins.

items.title: Will We Ever Find Alien Civilizations?
items.summary: Despite centuries of inquiry and decades of searching, the question of intelligent life elsewhere in the universe remains unanswered, with occasional intriguing signals yet to provide definitive proof. Scientific efforts continue to seek evidence for alien civilizations.

items.title: Introducing GPT‑Live
items.summary: OpenAI has upgraded ChatGPT's voice mode with GPT-Live, a new model that can delegate complex tasks to GPT-5.5 while maintaining conversation flow. The improved voice mode offers enhanced brainstorming capabilities and addresses previous issues, such as unexpected interruptions.

items.title: Quoting Kenton Varda
items.summary: Kenton Varda imposed a moratorium on AI-written change descriptions for his team, stating that they were "worse than useless" because they omitted the necessary high-level framing. AI-generated messages tended to focus on code details rather than the broader purpose, hindering effective code review.

items.title: Rewriting Bun in Rust
items.summary: Jarred Sumner detailed the process of rewriting Bun from Zig to Rust, a sophisticated project significantly aided by AI-powered agentic engineering, which automated much of the initial port. The rewrite, which cost an estimated $165,000 in tokens, improved startup speed and largely went unnoticed by users, highlighting the potential of LLMs for ambitious software development.

items.title: Jane Jacobs’s urbanism without economics
items.summary: A critical re-evaluation of Jane Jacobs's "The Death and Life of Great American Cities" argues that her urbanism lacks economic understanding, making her analyses of cause and effect in neighborhoods questionable. While appreciating her skepticism of parks and urban crowding, the critique highlights her inability to explain how contemporary cities develop or decline without economic context.

items.title: Mark Carney’s national liberalism
items.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's "national liberalism" is presented as a successful model for the center-left, with his Liberal Party maintaining high approval and securing a majority government after years in power. His approach offers lessons for other center-left parties globally that have struggled to govern effectively after winning elections.

items.title: Upzoned, overbid, under construction
items.summary: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, aimed at addressing housing issues, is set to become law automatically after Congress sent it to President Trump's desk last month without a formal veto. Meanwhile, the article notes homes are under construction in new housing developments in Valencia, California.

items.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
items.summary: Anthropic's new Claude Reflect dashboard visualizes user AI usage, subtly reinforcing the extent to which daily work depends on the chatbot. This feature goes beyond simple analytics by highlighting the user's reliance on Claude, potentially influencing continued engagement.

items.title: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plots major US expansion
items.summary: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plans a major US expansion, establishing an operations and manufacturing facility in Tulsa, Oklahoma, expected to create 1,000 jobs. This move signals significant growth for drone logistics in the American market.

items.title: Block reaches $45M settlement with 46 states over Cash App fraud probe
items.summary: Block has reached a $45 million settlement with 46 states following a probe into Cash App, which attorneys general found misled users by falsely advertising bank-like fraud protections. The investigation concluded that the popular payment app's claims about advanced fraud detection were deceptive.

items.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
items.summary: Character.AI is entering the microdrama arena with its own productions, featuring an interactive twist where users can chat with characters and roleplay storylines. This unique approach leverages the company's core product to offer an engaging and personalized entertainment experience.

items.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement
items.summary: A judge has approved Elon Musk's $1.5 million SEC settlement, bringing an end to the dispute over how he disclosed his growing stake in Twitter (now X). The approval came despite the judge's "misgivings" about the settlement terms.

items.title: Elon Musk says X will send DMs when posts you’ve engaged with are corrected
items.summary: Elon Musk announced that X will send direct messages to users when posts they've engaged with receive Community Notes, a crowdsourced fact-checking system. This update aims to address criticisms that the platform's misinformation correction often arrives too late.

items.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
items.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has demanded that autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders, emphasizing that emergency scenes are not "edge cases." This directive highlights growing concerns about the safe operation of AVs in complex real-world scenarios.

items.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
items.summary: Google Photos has introduced a new AI "Video Remix" tool that can apply cinematic relighting, swap backgrounds, or add artistic styles to videos. This feature enhances video editing capabilities with advanced artificial intelligence.

items.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
items.summary: Google's deepfake detector system was used to debunk an AI-generated hoax picture showing Senator Mitch McConnell in distress in a hospital bed. This demonstrates the system's utility in identifying fabricated images and combating misinformation.

items.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
items.summary: Lovable is reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2 billion, with a $300 million funding round expected to be led by Menlo Ventures. This potential investment highlights significant growth and investor confidence in the company.

items.title: Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
items.summary: Meta is adding a safeguard to its AI glasses to prevent secret recording, but this update comes amid concerns about the company's broader strategy of collecting and using personal data for its AI products. Critics argue Meta's AI ambitions may overshadow privacy considerations.

items.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
items.summary: Nandan Nilekani has stepped down from his General Partner role at Fundamentum as the firm launches its third $200 million fund, though he remains an anchor investor. Fundamentum is expanding its leadership and targeting AI and fintech startups in India.

items.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
items.summary: Ollama, a popular open-source AI developer tool that helps run AI on PCs, has raised $65 million, growing to nearly 9 million users. The Benchmark-backed company has gained significant traction, amassing 176,000 stars and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub.

items.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
items.summary: Startup General Intuition is betting that millions of hours of video game data can train foundational models for physical AI, potentially leading to a "ChatGPT moment" for robotics. This approach aims to create smarter robots with minimal real-world data, simplifying their development.

items.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules
items.summary: Truecaller is clashing with India's telecom regulator over anti-spam rules, as the caller ID company observes users increasingly ignoring and blocking calls from dedicated business number series. This conflict highlights challenges in balancing spam prevention with legitimate business communications.

items.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
items.summary: With EU backing, German startup QuantumDiamonds aims to accelerate chip manufacturing through a novel approach to inspecting semiconductors. Benefiting from the European Chips Act, the company focuses on improving chip industry processes with quantum technology.

items.title: A Way Out of the Iran Mess
items.summary: The article critiques the Trump administration's approach to Iran, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, linking its "stubborn refusal to confront reality" to the collapse of a deeply flawed memorandum of understanding. It draws a parallel to Churchill's warning about choosing "Shame" leading to "War" on worse terms.

items.title: Graham Platner Is Not Going Quietly
items.summary: Graham Platner ended his Senate campaign with a defiant video, blaming the "brutal political reality" and the establishment for stripping his campaign of essential resources after a rape allegation. He denied the accusations and portrayed himself as a victim of a system rigged against his movement, winning an "origin story" in the process.

items.title: The Iran the Funeral Cameras Don’t Show
items.summary: Despite carefully choreographed footage showing vast crowds mourning Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, sources inside Iran convey disbelief, relief, and joy following his death in a US-Israeli air strike. The article suggests state-broadcasted images do not reflect the true sentiments of many Iranians, who remain fearful but privately celebrate.

items.title: Tough Love: I Can’t Talk to My Wife About Money
items.summary: A husband describes a "vicious cycle" of money arguments with his wife, stemming from her overspending, inconsistent contributions to their joint account, and his anxiety about savings. Despite their agreement for her to contribute half her paycheck, she has stopped meeting the agreed amount, causing financial strain and marital conflict.

items.title: FL Studio 2026 turns its AI chatbot into your assistant engineer
items.summary: FL Studio 2026 integrates an enhanced AI chatbot, Gopher, which can now execute musical actions on behalf of the user, beyond just providing instructions. While it can lay down drum patterns and apply effects, Gopher still has limitations such as creating automation or inserting notes.

items.title: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
items.summary: Meta has released Muse Spark 1.1, an updated AI model now available to developers via a new Meta Model API, enhancing its capabilities for coding tasks. The company claims significant improvements in detecting and fixing complex bugs, supporting agentic workflows, and offering native multimodal perception.

items.title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
items.summary: Anthropic introduced a "reflect" feature for its Claude chatbot, allowing users to review their AI usage patterns, key topics, and task types over various periods. This "Claude Wrapped" feature offers a summary of individual AI interaction, aiming to help users understand and shape their habits.

items.title: Schlage’s Sense Pro unlocks the door so I don’t have to
items.summary: The Schlage Sense Pro is a new smart lock featuring Matter-over-Thread and hands-free unlocking via ultra-wideband (UWB) with Apple Home Key, promising faster and more reliable access. This $399 lock is Schlage's first without a physical keyhole and offers upcoming support for Google and Samsung.

items.title: The PocketMage resurrects the PDA with an e-paper screen
items.summary: Talisman Design is crowdfunding the PocketMage, a clamshell PDA that aims to resurrect the classic device with an e-paper screen, OLED display, and tactile keyboard. Available for preorder starting at $185, this device targets fans of physical interfaces and specialized gadgets.
